intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0) with scanpy using the standard QC → normalization → HVG → PCA → clustering → UMAP workflow.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Now let me present a plan for processing:

input.title: Process GSE192391 Patient 145 Day 0 with scanpy
input.summary: Standard scRNA-seq QC, normalization, feature selection, dimensionality reduction, clustering, and marker identification.

steps.title: Load the first sample (Day 0)
steps.description: Load GSM5746259 (Patient 145, Day 0) from the registered dataset

steps.title: QC metrics and filtering
steps.description: Calculate QC metrics (n_genes, n_counts, pct_mito), identify mitochondrial genes, and filter cells (n_genes ≥ 200, pct_mito < 0.20)
expected_outputs: QC metrics plots (n_genes, n_counts, pct_mito distributions)

steps.title: Normalization and log-transformation
steps.description: Normalize to 1e4 counts/cell and apply log1p transformation

steps.title: Highly variable genes and PCA
steps.description: Select HVGs and compute PCA (default 50 PCs)

steps.title: Neighborhood graph and UMAP
steps.description: Build k-nearest neighbor graph (default k=15) and compute UMAP

steps.title: Leiden clustering
steps.description: Leiden clustering on the k-nearest neighbor graph (default resolution 1.0)

steps.title: Marker genes and visualizations
steps.description: Rank genes per cluster and produce final UMAP + marker heatmap

assumptions: Processing the first sample (GSM5746259, Day 0) in isolation
Using default QC thresholds: n_genes ≥ 200, pct_mito < 0.20
PCA: 50 components; k-nearest neighbors: k=15; Leiden resolution: 1.0
Using scanpy (sc) for all steps; standard visualization with sc.pl.*
